author: tulas墨鈰卍膩sa
verses: 艣r墨鈰卹膩ma鈰卌andra k峁沺膩lu bhaju mana hara峁嘺 bhava鈰卋haya d膩ru峁嘺m nava鈰卥a帽ja鈰卨ocana, ka帽ja鈰卪ukha kara鈰匼nka帽ja pada鈰卥a帽j膩ru峁嘺m kandarpa aga峁噄ta amita chabi, nava鈰卬墨la鈰卬墨rada鈰卻undaram pa峁璦鈰卲墨ta m膩nahu ta岣峣ta ruci鈰吪泆ci naumi janaka sut膩鈰卾aram bhaju d墨na鈰卋andhu dine艣a d膩nava鈰匼ndaitya鈰卾a峁伵沘鈰卬ikandanam raghu鈰卬anda 膩nanda鈰卥anda kausala鈰匼ncanda da艣aratha鈰卬andanam 艣ira鈰卪uku峁璦 ku峁囜笉ala tilaka c膩ru ud膩ra a峁単a vibh奴峁峁嘺m 膩j膩nubhuja 艣ara鈰卌膩pa鈰卍hara sa艐gr膩ma鈰匼njita鈰卥hara鈰卍奴峁峁嘺m iti vadati tulas墨鈰卍膩sa 艣a艐kara鈰匼n艣e峁鈰卪uni鈰卪ana鈰卹a帽janam mama h峁沝aya鈰卥a帽ja niv膩sa kuru k膩m膩di鈰匼nkhala鈰卍ala鈰単a帽janam
en_translation:
O mind! Worship the merciful 艢r墨 R膩macandra, who removes the terrible fear of material existence. His eyes are like new lotuses, His face is like a lotus, His hands are like lotuses, and His feet are like reddish lotuses.

He has the limitless beauty of countless Cupids, beautiful like a fresh blue cloud. His yellow garment shines like lightning, I bow to the husband of the daughter of Janaka.

Worship the friend of the poor, the sun (of the solar dynasty), the destroyer of the demons and Diti's lineage. The delight of the Raghu dynasty, the root of bliss, the moon of Kau艣aly膩, the son of Da艣aratha.

Beautiful with a crown on His head, earrings, and tilaka; His generous limbs are adorned. With long arms reaching His knees, holding a bow and arrow, He conquered Khara and D奴峁峁嘺 in battle.

Thus says Tulas墨 D膩sa: O Lord who delights the minds of 艢a峁卥ara, 艢e峁, and the sages! Please reside in the lotus of my heart, destroying the group of wicked enemies headed by lust.
